pattern: Grouping digits in threes from the right is easiest done by reversing the digit string, chunking it, and reversing back -- no need to compute magnitude or divide repeatedly.
hints: Convert the number to its plain digit string first.
Working from the right end, split the digits into groups of three.
Join the groups back together (left to right) with a dot between each pair, being careful that the leftmost group may have fewer than three digits.

edge_inputs.description: fewer than four digits, no separator needed
smallest three-digit number, right at the no-separator boundary
smallest four-digit number, right at the separator boundary
leading group has exactly one digit before the first separator

stress.description: random integers across the full non-negative int32 range

complexity.time: O(d) where d is the number of digits
